case_metadata.case_name: John R. Orphanos, M.D. v. Michael Rodgers
case_metadata.court: Intermediate Court of Appeals of West Virginia

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: On June 4, 2017, Michael Rodgers, then 49 years old, was injured in a motorcycle crash. He was life-flighted to CAMC General in Charleston, a Level 1 Trauma Center.
evidence_index.summary: Mr. Rodgers was injured in a motorcycle crash and taken to a trauma center.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: A nurse practitioner from Dr. Orphanos’ practice first saw Mr. Rodgers at 11:30 a.m. on June 5, 2017. That nurse practitioner performed an initial assessment, placed him on spinal precautions, and ordered a thoracic lumbar sacral orthosis brace to be worn for a period of 6-8 weeks.
evidence_index.summary: Initial assessment and treatment plan for Mr. Rodgers by nurse practitioner.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Orphanos saw Mr. Rodgers later that afternoon and recommended a spinal surgical procedure as an alternative to the conservative treatment approach of the back brace.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Orphanos recommended surgery over conservative treatment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Postoperatively, Mr. Rodgers had no motor function or sensation below his nipple line.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: According to Dr. Orphanos’ experts, the paralysis was caused by a spinal cord infarct, which was neither predictable nor preventable.
evidence_index.summary: Paralysis attributed to spinal cord infarct by defense experts.
evidence_index.speaker: Defense expert

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The jury returned a verdict for Mr. Rodgers, finding that: (1) the first surgery was not an 'emergency surgery'; (2) Dr. Orphanos was negligent; (3) Dr. Orphanos’ breach of the standard of care caused or contributed to Mr. Rodgers’ paraplegia.
evidence_index.summary: Jury found Dr. Orphanos negligent and responsible for paraplegia.
evidence_index.speaker: Jury verdict

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: This Court concludes that the circuit court abused its discretion in denying Dr. Orphanos’ supplemental expert disclosure of Jodi A. Gehrig’s, M.D., testimony on Mr. Rodgers’ life expectancy which is relevant to the overall damages.
evidence_index.summary: Court found error in excluding expert testimony on life expectancy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Affirmed, in Part, and Reversed and Remanded, in Part.
evidence_index.summary: Court affirmed in part and reversed and remanded in part.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

verdict_detected.key_phrase: Affirmed, in Part, and Reversed and Remanded, in Part.

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient was involved in a motorcycle crash and was life-flighted to a trauma center.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

requestables.name: Orthopedic Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with Dr. Orphanos regarding spinal injury

reveal.clinical_significance: Surgical intervention proposed as alternative to conservative management
reveal.decision_impact: Influenced decision to proceed with surgery

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with spinal injury from motorcycle crash
decision_points.prompt: What treatment approach should be taken for the spinal injury?

options.clinical_reasoning: Surgery recommended by Dr. Orphanos as an alternative to conservative treatment

options.description: Continue with conservative management using a back brace
options.clinical_reasoning: Initial conservative treatment plan with brace was in place

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Surgery was recommended as an alternative

expected_action_court.description: Conservative management should have been continued
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Jury found surgery was not an emergency

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Surgery was not deemed necessary as an emergency procedure
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Conservative management should have been continued

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to trauma center after motorcycle crash

timeline_phases.name: Initial Assessment and Treatment Planning
timeline_phases.description: Assessment by nurse practitioner and consultation with Dr. Orphanos

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Decision to proceed with non-emergency surgery
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Conservative management could have avoided paraplegia
